test_name: Happy path login without MFA returns access token and sets httpOnly refresh cookie
preconditions: User 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' exists with status='active', role_code='RM', mfa_enabled=false
No Redis lockout key exists for this user
No prior failed login attempts
test_steps: 2. Capture the HTTP response status, body, and Set-Cookie headers
3. Inspect response body for access_token and mfa_required fields
4. Query audit_logs for action='login' and actor matching the user
expected_result: HTTP 200; response envelope { data: { access_token: '<jwt>', mfa_required: false }, meta: {}, error: null }; Set-Cookie header contains 'lms_refresh' with HttpOnly and Secure flags; audit_logs contains 1 row with action='login' and actor_id matching the user; users.last_login_at updated to within 5 seconds of now

test_name: Login for ADMIN (MFA-mandatory role) returns mfa_required=true and no access token
preconditions: User 'admin.sys@nbfc.local' exists with status='active', role_code='ADMIN', mfa_enabled=false (flag irrelevant — role mandates MFA)
TOTP secret seeded for this user in the test fixture
test_steps: 2. Assert response status and body structure
3. Assert that no access_token is present in the response
4. Assert Redis key 'mfa:challenge:<user_id>' exists with a TTL
expected_result: HTTP 200; data.mfa_required=true; data.mfa_challenge_token is a non-empty string; data.access_token is absent; Redis key mfa:challenge:<user_id> exists; no audit_logs(action='login') row yet (deferred until MFA step)
postconditions: Redis mfa:challenge key is set with TTL

test_name: MFA verify with correct TOTP issues access token and records login audit
preconditions: Login step completed for admin.sys@nbfc.local (TC-FR001-02), mfa_challenge_token captured
TOTP seed known; current 6-digit code generated from the seed
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and access_token in response
expected_result: HTTP 200; data.access_token is a valid JWT; data.mfa_required is absent or false; lms_refresh httpOnly cookie set; Redis mfa:challenge:<user_id> key is deleted; audit_logs has 1 row with action='login'; users.last_login_at updated
postconditions: Valid access token available for subsequent requests
MFA challenge key deleted from Redis

test_name: Refresh token rotation issues new access token and invalidates old refresh token
preconditions: User logged in as priya.rm@nbfc.local; valid lms_refresh cookie available
Access token NOT expired (this tests rotation, not silent-refresh-on-401)
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with the lms_refresh httpOnly cookie attached
2. Capture the new access_token and new lms_refresh cookie
3. Attempt POST /api/v1/auth/refresh again using the OLD lms_refresh value
4. Assert the second attempt is rejected
expected_result: First call: HTTP 200; new access_token returned; new lms_refresh cookie set; old refresh UUID is no longer in Redis. Second call with old cookie: HTTP 401 with error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED'
postconditions: Only the new refresh token is valid in Redis

test_name: Wrong password increments lockout counter and returns generic 401 without user enumeration
preconditions: User 'rajesh.bm@nbfc.local' exists with status='active', no prior failures in Redis
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 401; error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED'; error.message is generic (does not indicate whether username exists or password is wrong); Redis fail:<user_id>=1; audit_logs has action='login_failed'

test_name: Fifth consecutive wrong password triggers account lockout
preconditions: User 'rajesh.bm@nbfc.local' has Redis fail:<user_id>=4 (4 prior failures in Redis)
User status is 'active'
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error body
3. Query users table for status field
5. Verify audit_logs has login_failed with detail.reason='lockout_triggered'
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; error.detail.reason='ACCOUNT_LOCKED'; users.status='locked' in DB; Redis lockout:<user_id> key present; audit_logs has action='login_failed' with detail.reason='lockout_triggered'
postconditions: Subsequent login with correct password returns 403

test_name: Login on a locked account returns FORBIDDEN immediately without verifying password
preconditions: User 'rajesh.bm@nbfc.local' has status='locked' in the users table
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status, error code, and detail
4. Confirm argon2 verify was NOT called (mock spy assertion in unit test context)
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; error.detail.reason='ACCOUNT_LOCKED'; error.detail.retry_after_seconds is a positive integer; no access_token; audit_logs has action='login_failed'

test_name: Wrong OTP in MFA step returns 401 and preserves the challenge token for retry
preconditions: MFA challenge initiated for admin.sys@nbfc.local; mfa_challenge_token captured
Redis mfa:challenge:<user_id> exists
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error code
3. Confirm Redis mfa:challenge:<user_id> key still exists (not consumed)
expected_result: HTTP 401; error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED'; Redis mfa:challenge:<user_id> key still present; audit_logs has action='mfa_failed'; no access_token issued
postconditions: Challenge token still valid for retry

test_name: Replayed MFA challenge token is rejected after first use
preconditions: MFA challenge completed successfully for admin.sys@nbfc.local
The original mfa_challenge_token was consumed (Redis key deleted)
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/auth/mfa with the already-used mfa_challenge_token and a valid OTP
2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 401; error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED' (Redis key absent so token invalid); no second access_token issued

test_name: Expired refresh token returns 401 and does not issue new access token
preconditions: A refresh token UUID that is NOT present in Redis (expired or never issued)
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with a cookie 'lms_refresh=aaaaaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee' (not in Redis)
2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 401; error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED'; no new tokens issued

test_name: Rate limit on login endpoint returns 429 after 10 requests per minute
preconditions: Same IP address has made exactly 10 login requests within the past 60 seconds
test_steps: 1. Send the 11th POST /api/v1/auth/login from the same IP within 60 seconds
2. Assert HTTP status, error code, and Retry-After header
expected_result: HTTP 429; error.code='RATE_LIMITED'; Retry-After header is present with a positive integer value
postconditions: Request was not processed beyond rate-limit check

test_name: VALIDATION_ERROR returned when username is missing from login body
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status, error code, and fields array
expected_result: HTTP 400; error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'; error.fields contains an entry with field='username'

test_name: VALIDATION_ERROR returned when OTP is not 6 digits in MFA step
preconditions: A valid mfa_challenge_token exists in Redis
expected_result: HTTP 400; error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'; error.fields contains entry with field='otp'

test_name: Password reset returns identical 200 response whether email exists or not (anti-enumeration)
preconditions: User with email 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' exists
Email 'nobody@nowhere.invalid' does not exist
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/auth/reset with body { "email": "priya.rm@nbfc.local" } — record response body and status
2. POST /api/v1/auth/reset with body { "email": "nobody@nowhere.invalid" } — record response body and status
3. Compare both responses for any difference in status code, body structure, or field values
expected_result: Both calls return HTTP 200; response body is { data: null, meta: {}, error: null } for both; bodies are byte-identical; timing difference is less than 50 ms
postconditions: Redis pw_reset key set only for existing user
NotificationChannelPort called only for existing user

test_name: MFA is mandatory for PARTNER role regardless of mfa_enabled flag value
preconditions: User 'partner.user@dsa.com' exists with role_code='PARTNER', mfa_enabled=false
expected_result: HTTP 200; data.mfa_required=true; data.mfa_challenge_token present; no access_token in response

test_name: Audit logs never contain password hash, access token, refresh token, or OTP values
preconditions: Several successful and failed login attempts have been executed, generating audit_logs rows
test_steps: 1. Execute invariant SQL query against the test database
expected_result: Invariant query returns 0 rows

di_meta.setup_sql: INSERT INTO audit_logs (audit_id, org_id, actor_id, action, entity_type, created_at) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', gen_random_uuid(), 'login', 'users', now())
di_meta.action: Run successful and failed logins; then execute the invariant query
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs WHERE action IN ('login','login_failed','mfa_failed') AND (detail::text LIKE '%password%' OR detail::text LIKE '%access_token%' OR detail::text LIKE '%refresh_token%' OR detail::text LIKE '%otp%')
di_meta.cleanup: DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE created_at > :test_start AND org_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'

test_name: Locked user cannot appear as active in users table after lockout audit
preconditions: Lockout triggered for user 'rajesh.bm@nbfc.local' in the same test run
test_steps: 1. Trigger account lockout via 5 wrong-password attempts
2. Run invariant SQL to confirm no locked user shows status='active'
expected_result: Invariant query returns 0 rows; users.status='locked' for the tested user

di_meta.setup_sql: SELECT user_id FROM users WHERE username = 'rajesh.bm@nbfc.local'
di_meta.action: Perform 5 failed login attempts, then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT u.user_id FROM users u WHERE u.status = 'active' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM audit_logs al WHERE al.actor_id = u.user_id AND al.action = 'login_failed' AND al.detail->>'reason' = 'lockout_triggered' AND al.created_at > now() - interval '5 minutes')
di_meta.cleanup: UPDATE users SET status='active', version=version+1 WHERE username='rajesh.bm@nbfc.local'; DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE actor_id = (SELECT user_id FROM users WHERE username='rajesh.bm@nbfc.local') AND created_at > :test_start

test_name: Login form renders all required fields and is keyboard accessible
preconditions: Application is running; user is unauthenticated
test_steps: 2. Assert username and password inputs are visible
3. Assert submit button is present and enabled
4. Tab through the form to confirm keyboard navigation reaches all inputs
expected_result: Username input visible; password input visible; submit button visible; all inputs reachable via Tab key; no accessibility violations (axe)

ui_meta.visual_assert: Form is centered; inputs have visible labels

test_name: MFA challenge view appears after login with ADMIN credentials and accepts 6-digit OTP
preconditions: ADMIN user 'admin.sys@nbfc.local' exists with MFA configured
test_steps: 2. Enter username='admin.sys@nbfc.local' and password='Admin@5678!' and submit
3. Assert password form is replaced by OTP input without page reload
4. Assert 'Enter the 6-digit code' text is visible
5. Enter valid TOTP code and submit
expected_result: OTP input appears in place of password form; 'Enter the 6-digit code' text visible; valid TOTP leads to /dashboard; lms_refresh cookie is HttpOnly; access_token absent from localStorage and sessionStorage

ui_meta.visual_assert: Password form replaced by OTP form in same page without full reload
ui_meta.a11y: axe scan on MFA view — 0 violations

test_name: Refresh token is stored in httpOnly cookie — not in localStorage or sessionStorage
preconditions: RM user 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' credentials are valid
test_steps: 1. Log in as priya.rm@nbfc.local via the UI
2. After successful login, evaluate localStorage.getItem('access_token') in the browser
4. Check document.cookie for any token-like values
expected_result: localStorage.getItem('access_token') === null; sessionStorage.getItem('access_token') === null; document.cookie does not contain 'lms_refresh' (HttpOnly cookies are not accessible via JS); network response headers show Set-Cookie with HttpOnly flag

test_name: RM with scope O can view their own assigned lead

Lead LD-2026-000101 exists with owner_id=RM-A and stage='assigned'
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and data returned

expected_result: HTTP 200; data.lead_code='LD-2026-000101'; data.lead_identities.pan_masked='ABCxxxx4F' (not raw); data.lead_identities.mobile='98xxxxxx10' (masked)

test_name: RM receives 403 when attempting to view a lead owned by another RM
preconditions: RM-A ('priya.rm@nbfc.local') and RM-B ('suresh.rm@nbfc.local') both exist in the same branch
Lead LD-2026-000202 has owner_id=RM-B
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error code
3. Query audit_logs for an abac_deny event with actor_id=RM-A
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; audit_logs has action='abac_deny' with actor_id=RM-A and entity_id matching the lead

test_name: BM with scope B sees all leads in their branch including other RMs' leads
preconditions: BM 'rajesh.bm@nbfc.local' is in branch_id=BRANCH-001
Three leads exist all with branch_id=BRANCH-001, each assigned to a different RM
expected_result: HTTP 200; data array contains exactly 3 leads; all 3 have branch_id=BRANCH-001; mobile fields are masked as '98xxxxxx10'

test_name: PARTNER receives 404 (existence hidden) when requesting another partner's lead
preconditions: PARTNER user 'partner.dsa1@dsa.com' has partner_id=PARTNER-001
Lead LD-2026-000303 was submitted by PARTNER-002
expected_result: HTTP 404; error.code='NOT_FOUND' (existence hidden per PARTNER_CROSS_ACCESS rule — must NOT return 403 which would confirm existence)

test_name: ADMIN without break-glass grant receives 403 on lead list endpoint
preconditions: ADMIN user 'admin.sys@nbfc.local' has no active break_glass_grants
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN' (ADMIN has no standing lead-content access per auth-matrix.json)

test_name: DPO receives masked mobile (scope M) on lead list — no raw PII returned

At least 1 lead exists with mobile='9876543210'

expected_result: HTTP 200; every lead in data array has mobile='98xxxxxx10'; no lead has mobile='9876543210' (raw); pan_masked values are also masked; no Reveal button available without break-glass

test_name: Unauthenticated request to leads endpoint returns 401
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 401; error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED'

test_name: No duplicate (role_id, capability) entries exist in role_permissions
preconditions: Database is seeded with all roles and their permissions
test_steps: 1. Execute invariant SQL query checking for duplicate role_permissions rows
expected_result: Invariant query returns 0 rows

test_name: RM sees masked PAN on Lead 360 page — no Reveal button when canUnmask=false
preconditions: RM 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' is logged in

test_steps: 3. Locate the PAN field on the Lead 360 page
5. Assert no Reveal button is present
expected_result: PAN field displays 'ABCxxxx4F'; no button with aria-label containing 'Reveal' is present in the DOM

test_name: ADMIN navigating to /leads is shown a FORBIDDEN error state — not an empty list
preconditions: ADMIN user 'admin.sys@nbfc.local' is logged in with no break-glass grant
test_steps: 3. Assert the error state component is rendered
expected_result: Page renders ErrorState component with text like 'You don't have access to this.' — does not show empty table or loading spinner indefinitely

ui_meta.visual_assert: ErrorState visible; no data table rendered

test_name: ADMIN creates a break-glass grant with valid four-eyes fields — grant created in pending/active state
preconditions: ADMIN user 'admin.sys@nbfc.local' (user_id=ADMIN-A) is authenticated
DPO user 'dpo@nbfc.local' (user_id=DPO-B) exists and is active — will be approver
A third user ADMIN-C exists to be the grantee
BREAK_GLASS_MAX_WINDOW_HOURS=72 is configured
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/admin/break-glass with ADMIN-A's JWT and body { granteeId: ADMIN-C, approverId: DPO-B, reason: 'Incident INC-2026-0042: customer complaint audit required', scopeType: 'lead', scopeRef: '<lead_uuid>', validFrom: '<now>', validUntil: '<now+24h>' }
3. Query break_glass_grants in DB for the returned grant_id
4. Assert audit_logs has an event for the request

test_data.reason: Incident INC-2026-0042: customer complaint audit required

expected_result: HTTP 201; data.status='pending' (awaiting approval); data.grantee_id=ADMIN-C; data.approver_id=DPO-B; DB row in break_glass_grants with same IDs; approver_id != grantee_id constraint satisfied

test_name: Nominated approver activates the break-glass grant via approve endpoint
preconditions: Grant from TC-FR003-01 created with approver_id=DPO-B
DPO-B 'dpo@nbfc.local' is authenticated
expected_result: HTTP 200; data.status='active'; data.approver_id=DPO-B; DB shows grant_status='active'; audit_logs has action='break_glass_access' or 'grant_approved' with grant_id reference
postconditions: Grant is now active and usable within valid_from..valid_until window

test_name: Self-approval attempt returns FORBIDDEN — four-eyes constraint enforced at request time
preconditions: ADMIN user 'admin.sys@nbfc.local' (user_id=ADMIN-A) is authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/admin/break-glass with body where approverId = granteeId = ADMIN-A
2. Assert HTTP status and error code
3. Confirm no row was inserted into break_glass_grants
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; no row inserted into break_glass_grants

test_name: Wrong approver attempting to approve grant returns FORBIDDEN
preconditions: Grant created with approver_id=DPO-B
User DPO-C (a different DPO) is authenticated
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; grant status remains 'pending' in DB

test_name: VALIDATION_ERROR when break-glass window exceeds maximum configured hours
preconditions: BREAK_GLASS_MAX_WINDOW_HOURS=72 is configured
ADMIN-A is authenticated
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error fields
expected_result: HTTP 400; error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'; error.fields contains entry with field='validUntil' and message indicating the window exceeds the maximum allowed

test_name: VALIDATION_ERROR when reason field is blank
preconditions: ADMIN-A is authenticated
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP status and error fields
expected_result: HTTP 400; error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'; error.fields contains entry with field='reason'

test_name: ADMIN accesses lead content with active break-glass grant — access is audited
preconditions: Active break-glass grant exists for ADMIN-A covering lead_id=LEAD-001
ADMIN-A is authenticated
test_steps: 2. Assert HTTP 200 and lead data returned
3. Assert audit_logs has action='break_glass_access' with entity_id=LEAD-001 and detail.grant_id set
expected_result: HTTP 200; lead data returned; audit_logs row with action='break_glass_access', entity_id=LEAD-001, detail.grant_id=<grant_uuid>; every access under the grant is individually audited

test_name: Expired grant denies ADMIN lead access after valid_until passes
preconditions: A grant was created with valid_until set 1 minute in the past
The expiry sweep job has run (or the grant's valid_until is used for real-time check)
test_steps: 1. GET /api/v1/leads/LEAD-001 with ADMIN-A's JWT (grant is expired)
2. Assert HTTP status and error code
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; no lead data returned; no break_glass_access audit row for this request

test_name: Revoked grant denies ADMIN lead access immediately after revocation
preconditions: Active grant exists; revoke was called setting status='revoked'
ADMIN-A is authenticated
test_steps: 2. Immediately attempt GET /api/v1/leads/LEAD-001 with ADMIN-A's JWT
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; lead data not returned; revocation takes effect at next EntitlementService check

test_name: RM cannot access /admin/break-glass endpoint — FORBIDDEN
preconditions: RM user 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' is authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/admin/break-glass with RM's JWT and any valid body
expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN' (RM role has no break_glass capability in auth-matrix.json)

test_name: break_glass_grants table never has a row where approver_id equals grantee_id
preconditions: Several break-glass grants exist in the test DB including approved ones
expected_result: 0 rows returned — enforced by both application logic and DB CHECK constraint ck_break_glass_four_eyes

di_meta.setup_sql: INSERT INTO break_glass_grants (grant_id, org_id, grantee_id, approver_id, reason, scope_type, valid_from, valid_until, status, created_by, updated_by, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', gen_random_uuid(), gen_random_uuid(), 'test reason', 'lead', now(), now() + interval '1 hour', 'pending', gen_random_uuid(), gen_random_uuid(), now(), now())
di_meta.action: Create grants via API and then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT grant_id FROM break_glass_grants WHERE approver_id = grantee_id

test_name: No active break-glass grant should have valid_until in the past after expiry sweep
preconditions: Expiry sweep job has been triggered
At least one grant had valid_until set in the past
expected_result: 0 rows returned after sweep — all expired grants have status='expired', none remain 'active'

di_meta.setup_sql: UPDATE break_glass_grants SET status='active', valid_until = now() - interval '5 minutes' WHERE grant_id = :test_grant_id
di_meta.action: Trigger expiry sweep job, then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT grant_id FROM break_glass_grants WHERE status = 'active' AND valid_until <= now()

test_name: ADMIN successfully requests break-glass grant via UI modal — grant appears in approval queue

test_name: Idempotent replay with same Idempotency-Key returns original lead without creating a duplicate
preconditions: RM 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' authenticated

expected_result: First call: HTTP 201; second call: HTTP 200; data.lead_id is identical for both; DB has exactly 1 leads row for mobile '9812345678'; audit_logs count=1; event_outbox count=1

expected_result: HTTP 201; data.stage='captured'; data.channel_created_by='website'; no authentication required; branch auto-assigned based on pin_code routing

test_name: VALIDATION_ERROR when mobile is missing from lead create body
preconditions: RM 'priya.rm@nbfc.local' authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads with body that omits identity.mobile
expected_result: HTTP 400; error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'; error.fields contains entry with field='identity.mobile'

expected_result: HTTP 400; error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'; error.fields contains entry with field='source.partner_code'

test_name: CONFLICT returned when duplicate detection blocks a strong duplicate (same PAN + mobile)

Duplicate config action=blocked for strong confidence
RM authenticated
expected_result: HTTP 409; error.code='CONFLICT'; error.detail.reason='DUPLICATE_BLOCKED'; error.detail.matches is a non-empty array with confidence='strong'; no new lead row created in DB

expected_result: HTTP 403; error.code='FORBIDDEN'; no lead row created

test_name: Bulk import with partial failure creates valid rows and generates row-error CSV

CSV file with 3 rows: rows 1 and 3 valid, row 2 has invalid mobile '0000000000'
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads/import with BM's JWT and the CSV as multipart/form-data
5. Assert error CSV GCS reference is set; download and verify row 2 entry
expected_result: HTTP 202; data.status='queued'; after completion: import_jobs.success_rows=2; import_jobs.failed_rows=1; error CSV contains row 2 with column='identity.mobile', code='VALIDATION_ERROR', message describing the invalid format; 2 new lead rows in DB

test_name: Transaction rollback — no partial state when outbox emit fails mid-transaction
preconditions: OutboxService.emit is mocked to throw an error
RM authenticated
test_steps: 2. OutboxService throws during the UnitOfWork transaction
4. Assert no rows in leads, lead_identities, source_attributions, stage_history for this attempt

test_name: RATE_LIMITED on /public/leads after 10 requests from same IP within 60 seconds
preconditions: 10 successful requests made to /public/leads from the same IP within 60 seconds
test_steps: 1. Send 11th POST /api/v1/public/leads from the same IP
expected_result: HTTP 429; error.code='RATE_LIMITED'; Retry-After header present with positive integer value

test_name: Every lead created by FR-010 has exactly one source_attribution row
preconditions: At least 3 leads created via POST /api/v1/leads in this test run
test_steps: 1. Execute invariant SQL checking source_attribution count per lead
expected_result: 0 rows returned — every lead has exactly 1 source_attribution

di_meta.setup_sql: SELECT lead_id FROM leads ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5
di_meta.action: Create several leads, then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT l.lead_id FROM leads l WHERE l.created_at >= :test_start AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM source_attributions sa WHERE sa.source_attribution_id = l.source_attribution_id) <> 1
di_meta.cleanup: DELETE FROM leads WHERE created_at > :test_start AND org_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'

test_name: No duplicate customer_profile rows for same org_id and primary_mobile
preconditions: Multiple leads created for the same mobile number '9876543210'
test_steps: 1. Create 3 leads for mobile '9876543210' (second and third will link to existing customer_profile)
expected_result: 0 rows returned — customer_profiles has exactly 1 row for (org_id, primary_mobile='9876543210') regardless of how many leads were created for that mobile

di_meta.setup_sql: SELECT customer_profile_id FROM customer_profiles WHERE primary_mobile = '9876543210'
di_meta.action: Create multiple leads with same mobile, then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT org_id, primary_mobile, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM customer_profiles GROUP BY org_id, primary_mobile HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
di_meta.cleanup: DELETE FROM customer_profiles WHERE primary_mobile = '9876543210' AND org_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'

test_name: DSA and Dealer source leads always have non-null partner_id in source_attributions

2. Create a lead with source='Dealer' and partner_code='DEALER-001'
expected_result: 0 rows returned — no DSA or Dealer lead has partner_id=NULL in source_attributions

di_meta.setup_sql: SELECT sa.source_attribution_id FROM source_attributions sa WHERE sa.source IN ('DSA', 'Dealer') LIMIT 5
di_meta.action: Create DSA/Dealer leads then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT sa.source_attribution_id FROM source_attributions sa JOIN leads l ON l.source_attribution_id = sa.source_attribution_id WHERE sa.source IN ('DSA', 'Dealer') AND sa.partner_id IS NULL
di_meta.cleanup: N/A — checked in context of prior test data

test_name: Score and score_reasons are present in 201 response when lead is created with enriched fields

ProductConfig for CV active with pan_required_at='before_kyc'
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads with body including: name='Ramesh Kumar', mobile='9876543210', product_code='CV', source='Branch', pin_code='400001', requested_amount=1500000, preferred_language='Hindi', employment_type='salaried'
expected_result: HTTP 201; data.score is an integer in [0, 100]; data.score_reasons is a non-empty array of strings such as ['mobile_verified', 'pin_present', 'requested_amount_present', 'language_preference_set', 'employment_type_present']
postconditions: leads.score and leads.score_reasons written to DB

test_name: Scoring failure does not block lead creation — score is null in response
preconditions: ScoringService is mocked to throw an error
RM authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads with valid body (ScoringService will throw internally)
3. Assert data.score is null and data.score_reasons is null
4. Confirm lead row exists in DB with score IS NULL
expected_result: HTTP 201; data.score=null; data.score_reasons=null; lead row exists in DB; no 500 or INTERNAL_ERROR returned
postconditions: Pino logger called with level='error' and lead_id

test_name: pan_missing_penalty reason applied when PAN absent and product requires PAN at_capture
preconditions: ProductConfig for SBL has pan_required_at='at_capture' — wait, for scoring edge case, use a product where pan_missing_penalty applies when pan absent
RM authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads with no pan_token, product_code='SBL', mobile='9200001111'
3. Confirm score is lower than it would be with PAN present
expected_result: HTTP 201; data.score_reasons includes 'pan_missing_penalty'; score is reduced relative to a lead with PAN present (at least 15 points lower based on default weight)

test_name: source_high_rejection penalty applied for configured high-rejection source
preconditions: ConfigurationVersion has 'Telecalling' in the high-rejection source list
RM authenticated
expected_result: HTTP 201; data.score_reasons includes 'source_high_rejection'; score reduced by 10 compared to a same-data lead with source='Branch'

test_name: Score is clamped to 0 — never negative — when all penalty factors apply simultaneously
preconditions: All penalty conditions set: no PAN, high-rejection source, high-risk partner
ConfigurationVersion active with weights summing to more than 100 in penalties
RM authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads with source='Telecalling' (high-rejection), partner=high-risk, no PAN, no optional fields
expected_result: HTTP 201; data.score >= 0 (floor clamping); data.score_reasons is non-empty; score is not negative

test_name: Score is clamped to 100 — never exceeds maximum — when all positive factors apply
preconditions: All positive scoring factors present: PAN, pin_code, requested_amount, preferred_language, employment_type, asset_type, good partner
RM authenticated
test_steps: 1. POST /api/v1/leads with all enrichment fields filled: pan_token='ABCDE1234F', pin_code='560001', requested_amount=2500000, preferred_language='Kannada', employment_type='salaried', asset_type='new', source='Referral', partner quality_score=90
expected_result: HTTP 201; data.score <= 100 (ceiling clamping); all positive reason codes present in data.score_reasons

test_name: Score is re-evaluated and updated when a scoring-relevant field is PATCHed

RM owns this lead

expected_result: HTTP 200; data.score is a new integer higher than 40; data.score_reasons includes a reason code related to amount; DB leads.score updated

test_name: CONFLICT returned when PATCH uses stale expected_version (optimistic lock)

test_name: No lead has a score outside the valid range [0, 100]

di_meta.cleanup: N/A — read-only invariant; data from prior tests

test_name: No lead has score_reasons=null when score is non-null
preconditions: Multiple leads with non-null scores exist in DB
expected_result: 0 rows returned — every lead with a non-null score also has a non-null, non-empty score_reasons array

di_meta.setup_sql: SELECT lead_id FROM leads WHERE score IS NOT NULL LIMIT 5
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT lead_id FROM leads WHERE score IS NOT NULL AND (score_reasons IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(score_reasons) = 0) LIMIT 1

test_name: No lead has a score_reasons entry not in the known ScoreReasonCode enum
preconditions: Several leads with score_reasons populated
test_steps: 1. Execute invariant SQL query checking all reason codes
expected_result: 0 rows returned — all score_reason codes in all leads are from the known enum set

di_meta.setup_sql: SELECT lead_id FROM leads WHERE score_reasons IS NOT NULL LIMIT 5
di_meta.action: Run after scoring creates entries, then run invariant
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT lead_id, elem FROM leads, jsonb_array_elements_text(score_reasons) AS elem WHERE score_reasons IS NOT NULL AND elem NOT IN ('mobile_verified','pin_present','requested_amount_present','high_amount','language_preference_set','pan_present','pan_missing_penalty','partner_quality_good','partner_high_risk','source_high_rejection','customer_type_business','employment_type_present','asset_details_present') LIMIT 1
